<!-- Generated by SpielGantt agent scaffolding. Managed by the agent prepare command. -->

# Use SpielGantt

Use this skill as the only command reference for generated project agents.
Workflow skills must delegate concrete CLI syntax here.

## Ground Rules

- Preserve ordinary user files outside `.spielgantt/`.
- Treat the `spielgantt` CLI as the package authority. Rust/core creates,
  reads, understands, mutates, controls, validates, and describes SpielGantt
  packages.
- Use CLI JSON contracts to inspect package semantics. Do not infer dependency
  validity, legal dependency choices, event references, validation state,
  file-format behavior, or mutation rules from GUI rendering.
- GUI visual layout, timeline row/lane placement, connector drawing, and
  interaction state are presentation concerns; do not treat them as package
  semantics.
- Treat task IDs and event IDs as user-readable canonical names that must be
  valid single filesystem path components.
- Treat task IDs and event IDs as one shared namespace.
- Prefer JSON inspection output before making changes.
- Validate after every mutation set.
- Use the recorded local CLI path from `.spielgantt/agent.json` when present;
  otherwise discover it from `spielgantt agent runtime --json`.

## Inspect

- `spielgantt agent prepare --json [PATH]`
- `spielgantt agent runtime --json`
- `spielgantt agent status --json [PATH]`
- `spielgantt agent snapshot --json [PATH]`
- `spielgantt validate --json [PATH]`
- `spielgantt task adoptable-folders --json`
- `spielgantt task list --json`
- `spielgantt task show --json <TASK_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task relationships --json`
- `spielgantt task workflow --json`
- `spielgantt event list --json`

Use `agent snapshot --json` first for setup and update work. It returns project
paths, readiness, validation, tasks, events, and dependencies. It exits
nonzero when the project is invalid; use `agent status --json` or
`validate --json` to inspect invalid projects.

## Mutate

- `spielgantt init [PATH]`
- `spielgantt task create <TASK_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task adopt --id <TASK_NAME> <TASK_FOLDER>`
- `spielgantt task insert-before <SELECTED_TASK_NAME> <TASK_NAME> --json`
- `spielgantt task insert-after <SELECTED_TASK_NAME> <TASK_NAME> --json`
- `spielgantt task rename <TASK_NAME> <TASK_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task delete <TASK_NAME> [--remove-from-chart|--delete-directory] [--json]`
- `spielgantt task update <TASK_NAME> --status <blocked|unblocked|done>`
- `spielgantt event create <EVENT_NAME>`
- `spielgantt event rename <EVENT_NAME> <EVENT_NAME>`
- `spielgantt event delete <EVENT_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task depend <TASK_NAME> <TASK_OR_EVENT_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task dependency remove <TASK_NAME> <BLOCKER_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task ends-at <TASK_NAME> <EVENT_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task ends-at --clear <TASK_NAME>`
- `spielgantt project update-readme --content <CONTENT> --expected-version <README_VERSION> --json`
- `spielgantt normalize [--apply] [PATH]`
- `spielgantt cache rebuild [PATH]`

Use `normalize` without `--apply` as a preview. Apply only after the user agrees
to folder renames.

## Review And Repair

- `spielgantt validate [--json] [PATH]`
- `spielgantt repair [PATH]`
- `spielgantt export [PATH]`
- `spielgantt task readme --open --dry-run <TASK_NAME>`
- `spielgantt task open --dry-run <TASK_NAME>`

Prefer dry-run open commands unless the user explicitly asks to launch an OS
opener.

## JSON Contracts

Stable JSON payloads exist for validation, task lists, task details, event
lists, dependency relationships, event-axis workflow, relative task inserts,
task deletion, project README updates, agent runtime, agent status, and agent
snapshot. Use those payloads instead of scraping human-readable output.

Unspecified task state fields are `null`. Dependency references may point to
tasks or events. `ends_at` may only point to an event. SpielGantt does not
maintain task-link metadata; ordinary files, filesystem links, aliases, or
shortcuts inside project folders remain user-owned content outside the package
contract.
